props.title: Tweet Drafts: Polymarket TN/WB Calibration Split

props.text: 1a. Same day, same venue. TVK 7c→99c. BJP 51→99. Calibration isn't market-level, it's venue-level.
1b. $22M priced TVK at 6.95¢. Closed 99.65¢. The prior was the bug, not the data.
2a. Polymarket got Bengal because Phalodi priced it too. Missed Tamil Nadu by 92pp because every Indian book ran the 1967 prior. Two venues, one blind spot.
2b. Single-venue confidence is a feature to fade. $22M priced TVK at 7¢ on a binary that asked "DMK win." The structural question was "can a debut party clear duopoly."

3a. Polymarket priced TVK at 6.95¢ on $22M of opinion. Closed 99.65¢. The market said "DMK win." The structural question was "can a debut party clear a duopoly held since 1967." Same ticker, different problem. The title was the bug.

3b. WB Polymarket: 51% → 99.55% in 24h, Phalodi priced same direction independently, late flip held. TN Polymarket: TVK 6.95¢ → 99.65¢ on $22M, every Indian book shared the 1967 Dravidian prior. One was different evidence. The other was the same blind spot.

4a. CalibrationGap update from today's tape. Polymarket WB flipped 51% → 99.55% in 24h on the BJP take. Phalodi Satta Bazar priced it the same way. Two venues, same direction, late move held. Tamil Nadu did the opposite: $22M priced TVK at 6.95¢, closed 99.65¢. Every Indian book ran the same 1967 Dravidian-duopoly prior, including the Polymarket version. Convergence is only signal when venues price different evidence. Shared continuity prior is a shared blind spot. Ingest resolution text and structural priors, not titles and consensus probabilities.

4b. A pseudonymous "orangexyz" called TVK on Polymarket by knocking on doors of watchman, friends, relatives, workers in Tamil Nadu. Bio: "VETRI NICHAYAM." Made him 80k overnight. $22M market priced TVK at 6.95¢, closed 99.65¢. "KairosHunter" recovered $50k+ in losses catching the BJP flip on Bengal — Polymarket 51% → 99.55%, Phalodi priced the same direction. Two PM resolutions. One day. The information was on the ground in TN or in turnout-phase data in WB. Neither is in the order book.

5a. Polymarket closed two Indian state markets today. WB flipped late and held: 51% → 99.55%. TN missed by 92pp: TVK 7¢ → 99¢ on $22M of priced opinion. Same day, same operator, opposite outcomes. Why?

5b. Today's $22M Polymarket TN miss isn't a calibration story. It's a convergence story. Cross-venue convergence is only signal when venues price different evidence. Bengal was free convergence. Tamil Nadu was free blind spot.

props.title: Recommendation
props.message: best: #4a — both data points, lands the operator-grade lesson, ends with the actionable upgrade
